rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE BANKS VS. APARTMENT INVESTMENT AND MANAGEMENT COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Bifurcate Issue Or Trial Phase As To Punitive Damages From Liability Phase
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 6, 2017 line 1. DEFENDANTs APARTMENT INVESTMENT AND MANAGEMENT COMPANY, ALL HALLOWS PRESERVATION, L.P., and NHPMN MANAGEMENT, L. P.'s MOTION To Bifurcate Issue Or Trial Phase As To Punitive Damages From Liability Phase OFF CALENDAR per request of the moving party.= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: KINSON WONG VS. JOE LING ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 6, 2017 line 2. DEFENDANTs THOMAS WONG, C.K. KWAN, EDMOND HON, 633 PARTNERSHIP, and 633 HOLDINGS, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts supporting his standing for him, as an individual, to get back interest from Ling. If Plaintiff is alleging derivative claims seeking to get Ling's interest back into the R&G Lounge, Inc., he must so allege. If Plaintiff is seeking to unwind the whole transaction and get back100% interest, as an individual, Plaintiff shall bring the R&G Lounge, Inc. and Hung into this action on appropriate causes of action. Further Plaintiff relies on Pearson v. Norton (1964) 230 Cal.App.2d 1 in support of his claims regarding individual partners' liability. However, Plaintiff fails to allege facts to support a necessary element, specifically, that the "fruits of [Ling's fraud] inured to the [partner's] benefit." Plaintiff must so allege in order to proceed against the individual partners. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: YURI GUEVARA ET AL VS. GRACE TANG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 6, 2017 line 3. DEFENDANTs GRACE TANG, VERNON TANG, ALEX TANG, and VICTORIA CORDEIRO's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT DENIED. The Complaint as alleged does not assert any claims arising out of protected activity. The Court notes that the moving party failed to analyze the complaint and allegations in it in accordance with the controlling law, Baral v. Schnitt (2016) 1 Cal.5th 376. The Court further notes that moving parties failed to identify in their moving papers any allegations in the First Amended Complaint allegedly based on protected activity. Such attempt is made in the reply without any analysis or reference to the controlling authority. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MURPHY INVESTMENTS, INC. VS. MARITZA QUEVEDO

Defendant to provide copies of the discovery she seeks to propound on Plaintiff (e.g. notice of deposition, interrogatories, etc.) to the Court no later than 9:00 a.m. on November 6, 2017. = (501/REQ)
